creator: jcfyecrayz@liamekaens.com
text:
@pi,

When you added extra-patch-lib_libfilezilla_string.hpp, PORTREVISION for ftp/libfilezilla was not bumped.  I ran into a situation where libfilezilla was built on a stable/13 system (before llvm was updated to 19 on stable/13).  Recently, after the system was updated to a more recent stable/13, ftp/filezilla got a PORTREVISION bump, so that triggered a rebuild of that port.  It started getting this error:

/usr/include/c++/v1/string_view:300:42: error: implicit instantiation of undefined template 'std::char_traits<unsigned char>'
In file included from filezilla.h:1:
In file included from ./../include/libfilezilla_engine.h:12:
In file included from /usr/local/include/libfilezilla/libfilezilla.hpp:7:
In file included from /usr/local/include/libfilezilla/string.hpp:10:
In file included from /usr/include/c++/v1/string:647:
/usr/include/c++/v1/string_view:300:42: error: implicit instantiation of undefined template 'std::char_traits<unsigned char>'
  300 |   static_assert(is_same<_CharT, typename traits_type::char_type>::value,
      |                                          ^

After applying extra-patch-lib_libfilezilla_string.hpp directly to include/libfilezilla/string.hpp, the build of ftp/filezil.la completes successfully.

I know it's a little late, and few people would be affected by this, but consider bumping PORTREVISION for ftp/libfilezilla to handle this [unusual perhaps, but quite legitimate] edge case.
